original_abstract: This research explores the degradation mechanisms of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) within alkaline electrolytes, facilitating "indirect" electrooxidation at exceptionally high substrate and base concentrations. By optimizing these parameters, the process achieves significantly improved atom economy, offering a more efficient pathway for the electrochemical conversion of biomass-derived compounds.
humanized_abstract: We investigate the factors determining the degradation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) in the electrolyte solution enabling the proposed “indirect” electrooxidation under extremely high substrate and base concentrations. This approach allows to gain a much higher atom economy thereby transforming more of the biomass derived organic molecules during electrolysis.
